text.title: Ruby on Rails Framework Fundamentals Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Rails apps.

antipattern.symptom: Running `Order.find(1).touch` in console sends production email; tests trigger emails to real addresses.
antipattern.fix: Move to OrderService#confirm which explicitly calls OrderMailer.confirmation(@order).deliver_later. Remove callback.

antipattern.symptom: User can set is_admin=1 via params; attacker becomes admin.
config/routes.rb is 400 lines; helpers don't exist for half the routes; named URLs are inconsistent.
antipattern.fix: Refactor into resources blocks: `resources :orders do member { patch :cancel } end`; named helpers (cancel_order_path) appear automatically.

antipattern.symptom: Sentry error includes Stripe key in error context because ENV is printed in 5xx page.
antipattern.root-cause: Stripe key read from ENV; ENV dump on error includes it.
antipattern.fix: Move STRIPE_SECRET to credentials.yml.enc; access via Rails.application.credentials.stripe[:secret]; ENV no longer holds it.
